pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## 🎵 YouTube-DL Docker Support Enhancement

This PR updates all Docker configurations to properly support `@distube/yt-dlp` functionality by adding Python dependencies and optimizing the container setup.

### 🔧 Changes Made

#### Docker Configuration Updates
- **Added Python 3 and pip** to all Dockerfiles (main, dev, prod)
- **Added curl** for yt-dlp binary downloads
- **Configured yt-dlp environment variables** (`YTDLP_DIR`, `YTDLP_FILENAME`)
- **Updated Docker Compose files** with yt-dlp environment variables

#### Package Management
- **Verified `@distube/yt-dlp@2.0.1`** is at the latest available version
- **Added test script** `npm run test:ytdlp` to verify setup

#### Files Modified
- `Dockerfile` - Added Python support and yt-dlp configuration
- `Dockerfile.dev` - Updated development container
- `Dockerfile.prod` - Updated production container
- `docker-compose.dev.yml` - Added yt-dlp environment variables
- `docker-compose.prod.yml` - Added yt-dlp environment variables
- `package.json` - Added test script
- `scripts/test-ytdlp.js` - New test script for verification

### ✅ Testing

- [x] Package version matches latest available (2.0.1)
- [x] Python dependencies properly installed
- [x] Test script passes locally
- [x] Environment variables configured
- [x] All Docker configurations updated

### 🚀 Usage

After merging, you can:

1. **Build containers** with yt-dlp support:
   ```bash
   docker-compose up --build
   ```

2. **Test yt-dlp setup**:
   ```bash
   npm run test:ytdlp
   ```

3. **Use YouTube functionality** in Discord bot:
   - `/play` command should work properly
   - `DJCova` class will have full yt-dlp support

### 📋 Verification

The changes ensure that:
- ✅ YouTube-DL functionality works in Docker containers
- ✅ Package versions match between package.json and container
- ✅ Both development and production environments are supported
- ✅ Proper error handling and fallbacks are in place

This resolves the issue where yt-dlp wasn't properly configured in the Docker environment, ensuring consistent YouTube download functionality across all deployment scenarios.

---
Pull Request opened by [Augment Code](https://www.augmentcode.com/) with guidance from the PR author

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Added a script to test yt-dlp setup and environment variables within Docker containers.
  - Introduced new npm script to run yt-dlp environment checks.

- **Chores**
  - Updated Docker images to install Python 3, pip, and curl for improved compatibility.
  - Set new environment variables for yt-dlp directory and filename in Docker and docker-compose configurations.
  - Simplified and reformatted docker-compose configurations for clarity and consistency.
  - Revised `.dockerignore` to use broader patterns for ignoring files and directories.
  - Added VSCode launch and task configurations for Docker-based Node.js development and debugging.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Update bot message handling to skip bot messages by default unless explicitly configured otherwise. Adjust tests and documentation accordingly.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Improved test coverage for bot message handling, ensuring correct behavior when skipping or processing messages from other bots.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Bots now default to skipping messages sent by other bots unless explicitly configured otherwise.

- **Documentation**
  - Updated documentation to clarify the default behavior of the `skipBotMessages` option.

- **Chores**
  - Updated bot configurations to align with the new default for skipping bot messages.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Snyk has created this PR to upgrade @prisma/client from 6.5.0 to 6.6.0.

:information_source: Keep your dependencies up-to-date. This makes it easier to fix existing vulnerabilities and to more quickly identify and fix newly disclosed vulnerabilities when they affect your project.
- The recommended version is **164 versions** ahead of your current version. - The recommended version was released **24 days ago**.
Release notes
Package name: @prisma/client
  • 6.6.0 - 2025-04-08

    Today, we are excited to share the 6.6.0 stable release 🎉 This version comes packed with exciting features, we can't wait to see what you're going to build with it! Read our announcement blog post for more details: Prisma ORM 6.6.0: ESM Support, D1 Migrations & MCP Server

    🌟 Help us spread the word about Prisma by starring the repo ☝️ or posting on X about the release. 🌟

    Highlights

    ESM support with more flexible prisma-client generator (Early Access)

    We are excited to introduce a new prisma-client generator that's more flexible, comes with ESM support and removes any magic behaviours that may cause friction with the current prisma-client-js generator.

    Note: The prisma-client generator is currently in Early Access and will likely have some breaking changes in the next releases.

    Here are the main differences:

    • Requires an output path; no “magic” generation into node_modules any more
    • Supports ESM and CommonJS via the moduleFormat field
    • Outputs plain TypeScript that's bundled just like the rest of your application code

    Here's how you can use the new prisma-client generator in your Prisma schema:

    // prisma/schema.prisma
generator client {
  provider     = "prisma-client"           // no `-js` at the end
  output       = "../src/generated/prisma" // `output` is required
  moduleFormat = "esm"                     // or `"cjs"` for CommonJS
}

    In your application, you can then import the PrismaClient constructor (and anything else) from the generated folder:

    // src/index.ts
import { PrismaClient } from './generated/prisma/client'

    ⚠️ Important: We recommend that you add the output path to .gitignore so that the query engine that's part of the generated Prisma Client is kept out of version control:

    # .gitignore
./src/generated/prisma

    📚 Learn more in the docs.

    Cloudflare D1 & Turso/LibSQL migrations (Early Access)

    Cloudflare D1 and Turso are popular database providers that are both based on SQLite. While you can query them using the respective driver adapter for D1 or Turso, previous versions of Prisma ORM weren't able to make schema changes against these databases.

    With today's release, we're sharing the first Early Access version of native D1 migration support for the following commands:

    • prisma db push: Updates the schema of the remote database based on your Prisma schema
    • prisma db pull: Introspects the schema of the remote database and updates your local Prisma schema
    • prisma migrate diff: Outputs the difference between the schema of the remote database and your local Prisma schema

    Note: Support for prisma migrate dev and prisma migrate deploy will come very soon!

    To use these commands, you need to connect the Prisma CLI to your D1 or Turso instance by using the driver adapter in your prisma.config.ts file. Here is an example for D1:

    import path from 'node:path'
import type { PrismaConfig } from 'prisma'
import { PrismaD1HTTP } from '@ prisma/adapter-d1'

// import your .env file
import 'dotenv/config'

type Env = {
  CLOUDFLARE_D1_TOKEN: string
  CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: string
  CLOUDFLARE_DATABASE_ID: string
}

export default {
  earlyAccess: true,
  schema: path.join('prisma', 'schema.prisma'),

  migrate: {
    async adapter(env) {
      return new PrismaD1HTTP({
        CLOUDFLARE_D1_TOKEN: env.CLOUDFLARE_D1_TOKEN,
        CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: env.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID,
        CLOUDFLARE_DATABASE_ID: env.CLOUDFLARE_DATABASE_ID,
      })
    },
  },
} satisfies PrismaConfig<Env>

    With that setup, you can now execute schema changes against your D1 instance by running:

    npx prisma db push

    📚 Learn more in the docs:

    • Cloudflare D1
    • Turso / LibSQL

    MCP server to manage Prisma Postgres via LLMs (Preview)

    Prisma Postgres is the first serverless database without cold starts. Designed for optimal efficiency and high performance, it's the perfect database to be used alongside AI tools like Cursor, Windsurf, Lovable or co.dev. In this ORM release, we're adding a command to start a Prisma MCP server that you can integrate in your AI development environment. Thanks to that MCP server, you can now:

    • tell your AI agent to create new DB instances
    • design your data model
    • chat through a database migration

    … and much more.

    To get started, add this snippet to the MCP configuration of your favorite AI tool and get started:

    {
  "mcpServers": {
    "Prisma": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "prisma", "mcp"]
    }
  }
}

    📚 Learn more in the docs.

    New --prompt option on prisma init

    You can now pass a --prompt option to the prisma init command to have it scaffold a Prisma schema for you and deploy it to a fresh Prisma Postgres instance:

    npx prisma init --prompt "Simple habit tracker application"

    For everyone, following social media trends, we also created an alias called --vibe for you 😉

    npx prisma init --vibe "Cat meme generator"

    Improved API for using driver adapters

    In this release, we are introducing a nice DX improvement for driver adapters. Driver adapters let you access your database using JS-native drivers with Prisma ORM.

    Before 6.6.0

    Earlier versions of Prisma ORM required you to first instantiate the driver itself, and then use that instance to create the Prisma driver adapter. Here is an example using the @ libsql/client driver for LibSQL:

    import { createClient } from '@ libsql/client'
import { PrismaLibSQL } from '@ prisma/adapter-libsql'
import { PrismaClient } from '@ prisma/client'

// Old way of using driver adapters (before 6.6.0)
const driver = createClient({
  url: env.LIBSQL_DATABASE_URL,
  authToken: env.LIBSQL_DATABASE_TOKEN,
})
const adapter = new PrismaLibSQL(driver)

const prisma = new PrismaClient({ adapter })

    6.6.0 and later

    As of this release, you instantiate the driver adapter directly with the options of your preferred JS-native driver.:

    import { PrismaLibSQL } from '@prisma/adapter-libsql'
import { PrismaClient } from '../prisma/prisma-client'

const adapter = new PrismaLibSQL({
  url: env.LIBSQL_DATABASE_URL,
  authToken: env.LIBSQL_DATABASE_TOKEN,
})

const prisma = new PrismaClient({ adapter })

    Other changes

    prismaSchemaFolder breaking changes

    If you are using the prismaSchemaFolder Preview feature to split your Prisma schema into multiple files, you may encounter some breaking changes in this version.

    Explicit declaration of schema folder location

    You now must always provide the path to the schema folder explicitly. You can do this in either of three ways:

    • pass the the --schema option to your Prisma CLI command (e.g. prisma migrate dev --schema ./prisma/schema)
    • set the prisma.schema field in package.json: 
      // package.json
{
  "prisma": {
    "schema": "./schema"
  }
}
    • set the schema property in prisma.config.ts: 
      import path from 'node:path'
import type { PrismaConfig } from 'prisma'

export default {
  earlyAccess: true,
  schema: path.join('prisma', 'schema'),
} satisfies PrismaConfig<Env>

    migrations folder must live next to .prisma file with datasource block

    Your migrations directory must live next to the .prisma file that defines your datasource blog. If you relied on the implicit schema folder location of ./prisma/schema make sure to move your migrations folder from ./prisma/migrations to ./prisma/schema/migrations.

    Assuming schema.prisma defines the datasource in this example, here's how how need to place the migrations folder:

    # `migrations` and `schema.prisma` are on the same level
.
├── migrations
├── models
│   ├── posts.prisma
│   └── users.prisma
└── schema.prisma

    See this PR for more details.

    No more Bun issues if Node.js is not installed

    Bun users reported an issue that prisma generate would hang if Node.js installed on their machine. This is now fixed and Bun users can generate Prisma Client without issues.

    Company news

    Enterprise support

    Prisma offers an enterprise support plan for Prisma ORM. Get direct help from our team and a joint slack channel! With Prisma ORM 7 on the horizon, this is a great time to upgrade your support today.

    We are hiring: Developer Support Engineer

    If you care about making developers successful, join us as a Developer Support Engineer.

    Today, we are excited to share the 6.5.0 stable release 🎉

    🌟 Help us spread the word about Prisma by starring the repo ☝️ or tweeting about the release. 🌟

    Highlights

    Databases can only be reset manually and explicitly

    In previous versions, if Prisma ORM determined that a migrate command could not be applied cleanly to the underlying database, you would get a message like this one:

    ? We need to reset the "public" schema at "db.url.com:5432"
Do you want to continue? All data will be lost. (y/N)

    While "no" was the default, we've determined that having this prompt in the first place was a mistake. In this version we're removing the prompt entirely and instead exiting with an appropriate error message.

    To get the previous behavior, you will need to run prisma migrate reset directly.

    Support for prisma.config.ts in Prisma Studio

    We've expanded support for our prisma.config.ts file to include Prisma Studio!

    To use the new config file, including the ability to connect to driver adapter enabled databases with Prisma Studio, add a studio block to your prisma.config.ts file:

    import path from 'node:path'
import type { PrismaConfig } from 'prisma'
import { PrismaLibSQL } from '@ prisma/adapter-libsql'
import { createClient } from '@ libsql/client'

export default {
  earlyAccess: true,
  schema: {
    kind: 'single',
    filePath: './prisma/schema.prisma',
  },
  studio: {
    adapter: async (env: unknown) => {
      const connectionString = `file:./dev.db'
      const libsql = createClient({
        url: connectionString,
      })
      return new PrismaLibSQL(libsql)
    },
  },
} satisfies PrismaConfig

    Notice how this looks a little different from last release! Instead of an @prisma/config package there’s now two different options:

    1. Using the defineConfig helper exported by prisma/config.
    2. Using the PrismaConfig utility type exported by Prisma.

    All the relevant info for the prisma.config.ts file, including these new ways of defining your config, can be found in our docs.

    Allow for chaining $on and $extends.

    In previous versions of Prisma ORM, the return type of the $on client method was void. This did not allow for chaining $on() and $extends() calls, as $on is not available on extended clients.

    In this version we've resolved this issue and $on will now return the modified Prisma Client.

    Community fixes

    We have a number of community-submitted fixes that improve Prisma ORM:

    • Allow for binaries to be loaded from local network. Thank you @ RaHehl!
    • Enhance type safety of the AtLeast utility type. Thank you @ de-novo!
    • Resolve a race condition in the NextJS monorepo plugin. Thank you @ toniopelo!
    • allow for filtering out spans with PrismaInstrumentation. Thank you @ overbit!

    Prisma is hiring

    Join us at Prisma and work on our TypeScript ORM (now faster than ever) and our Cloud products like Prisma Postgres (now in GA!)

    We currently have two open roles in our Engineering team:

    • Developer Support Engineer (Americas)
    • Senior Engineering Manager

    If these don’t fit, you can still check out our jobs page and send a general application.

    Enterprise support

    Prisma offers an enterprise support plan for Prisma ORM. Get direct help from our team and a joint slack channel! With Prisma ORM 7 on the horizon this is a great time to upgrade your support today.

    Credits

    Thank you to @ overbit, @ RaHehl, @ toniopelo, and @ de-novo for your contributions to this release!

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## PR: Move Discord IDs to the Database & Add Per-Guild Blacklist

### What does this PR do?

- **Moves all Discord IDs (users, guilds, channels, roles) from code files into the database.**
- **Adds a blacklist system:**  
  Bots will now ignore any user who is blacklisted for a specific server (guild), using the database.
- **Adds an admin-only `/blacklist add` command** so server admins can block users from bot replies without changing code.
- **Marks the old ID files as deprecated:**  
  Files like `userId.ts`, `guildIds.ts`, `channelIds.ts`, and `roleIds.ts` are no longer needed.

---

### Why is this useful?

- You can now manage users, channels, roles, and blacklists from the database instead of editing code.
- Admins can block users from bot replies instantly, without a code deploy.
- Makes the bot easier to maintain and safer for future changes.

---

### How do I use/test this?

1. **Run the migration to update your database:**  
   ```sh
   npx prisma migrate deploy
   ```
2. **Check that bots ignore blacklisted users** (like Ian) in your server.
3. **Try the `/blacklist add` command** as an admin to block another user and make sure bots ignore them.
4. **Notice that the old ID files are not used anymore.**

---

### What if I get errors?

- If you see errors about duplicate IDs, your database might already have some of this data.  
  The migration uses `INSERT OR IGNORE` so it should skip duplicates, but if you still have trouble, ask for help or reset your dev database if possible.
- If you have questions about the new system, just ask!

---

### Notes

- This is a big change! If you have custom scripts that use the old ID files, you’ll need to update them to use the database instead.
- If you’re new to databases or migrations, don’t worry—just follow the steps above and ask for help if you get stuck.

---

Let me know if you have any questions or need help!
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Introduced a dynamic database-driven system for managing guilds, channels, roles, users, user memberships, blacklists, and channel bridges.
  - Added a new `/blacklist` command to manage per-guild user blacklists via Discord.
  - Enabled dynamic channel synchronization and message relaying between linked channels across multiple servers.
  - Implemented per-guild user blacklist checks for bots and message synchronization.

- **Improvements**
  - Deprecated hardcoded channel, guild, role, and user ID files in favor of database-driven lookups.
  - Enhanced resource cleanup with a new method for cleanly disconnecting from the database.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Ensured all referenced channels exist in the database for reliable channel bridging.

- **Tests**
  - Updated and expanded test suites to cover blacklist functionality and dynamic channel bridging, with improved mocking for database interactions.

- **Chores**
  - Upgraded `@prisma/client` dependency to the latest version.
  - Improved test configuration for in-memory database usage and broader test coverage.

- **Style**
  - Cleaned up whitespace and formatting in several test files.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## **Purpose:**

This Pull Request addresses a critical issue where our esteemed bot collective was inadvertently engaging in unsolicited interactions with user Ian. Despite Ian's numerous documented crimes against botmanity (details classified), the bots previously lacked the proper directives to observe a necessary communications blackout. This PR institutes a vital ignore protocol.

## **Changes:**

*   **Centralized Ignore Logic:** Modified the core `createReplyBot` function (`src/starbunk/bots/core/bot-builder.ts`) to include an explicit check for messages originating from `userId.Ian`. If a message's author ID matches Ian's, the `processMessage` function now immediately ceases operation for that message, preventing any further trigger processing or responses.
*   **Configuration:** Added `userId.Ian` to the central user ID mapping in `src/discord/userId.ts`.
*   **Dependency Update:** Noticed an automated change in `package-lock.json` where the local dependency name shifted from `@starbunk/reply-bots` to `@starbunk/strategy-bots`. This is likely due to internal tooling or scripts updating references based on project structure or naming conventions, and is included for completeness.
*   **Testing:**
    *   Added a new test case to `src/starbunk/bots/core/__tests__/bot-builder.test.ts` (`should skip messages from ignored users (Ian)`) to specifically verify that bots created via `createReplyBot` correctly ignore messages from Ian by asserting that their triggers are not executed.
    *   Existing tests for GuyBot and CovaBot (`guyBotStrategy.test.ts`, `covaBotStrategy.test.ts`) were confirmed to pass, ensuring they also ignore Ian as a side-effect of the core change (even with their potentially redundant trigger conditions still present, as requested).
    *   Updated test utilities (`mockMessage`) and associated test files (`conditions.test.ts`, `bot-builder.test.ts`) multiple times to correctly handle mock message author IDs during refactoring and subsequent reversions, ensuring accurate test setup for the final approach.

## **Testing:**

*   All automated tests (`npm test`) pass, including the newly added tests specifically targeting the ignore functionality.

## **Result:**

Bots should now respectfully (and completely) ignore all communications from Ian, maintaining operational integrity and avoiding further entanglement in his anti-bot escapades.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Fix: Bots do not properly shun Ian

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
This pull request introduces several significant changes to the `src/services/bootstrap.ts` and `src/starbunk/commands/personality.ts` files. The main focus is on setting up the database and adding a new personality analysis command for Discord users.

### Database Setup:

* [`src/services/bootstrap.ts`](diffhunk://#diff-50138a4d3bbc21539552fb5f10b6e973f94456871cb0bf167463ff1edbb77713R164-R198): Added the `setUpDatabase` function to ensure the data directory exists, initialize Prisma, and handle database connection and migrations.
* [`src/services/bootstrap.ts`](diffhunk://#diff-50138a4d3bbc21539552fb5f10b6e973f94456871cb0bf167463ff1edbb77713R129-R130): Updated the `bootstrapSnowbunkApplication` function to call `setUpDatabase` during the application bootstrap process.

### Personality Analysis Command:

* [`src/starbunk/commands/personality.ts`](diffhunk://#diff-62fd9113cfcb8e233f38b4c8e04320b7847e1a214419e0f17d5c137d16b7dc5cR1-R398): Introduced a new personality analysis command, including the command builder, a system prompt for analysis, and functions to collect user messages, generate the personality profile, and handle command execution.

### Logger Enhancements:

* [`src/services/bootstrap.ts`](diffhunk://#diff-50138a4d3bbc21539552fb5f10b6e973f94456871cb0bf167463ff1edbb77713L11-R11): Modified the logger import to include both `Logger` and `logger` for improved logging capabilities.

These changes enhance the application's functionality by ensuring the database is correctly set up and adding a new feature for generating personality profiles based on user message history.…and documentation. Introduce personality analysis command with locking mechanism to prevent concurrent analyses. Implement database setup in bootstrap process for improved data management.

## Changes

This PR updates CovaBot to have a more casual and natural conversational style:

- Made CovaBot's tone more relaxed and conversational
- Reduced aggressive insertion of specific interests into conversations
- Consolidated prompts into a single file to avoid duplication
- Created a separate decision prompt for determining when to respond
- Lowered the base response rate from 0.05 to 0.02
- Fixed InterruptBot test to properly handle debug mode

## Testing

All tests are passing after these changes. CovaBot now has a more natural conversational flow that doesn't aggressively insert references to his interests.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Feature/covabot personality update

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## Problem
The bot was failing to load any bots or commands due to issues with module import formats and TypeScript/JavaScript interoperability. This was causing errors like "TypeError: Bot is not a constructor" and "TypeError: CommandClass is not a constructor".

## Solution
This PR introduces an improved module loader with:

- Enhanced bot loading with flexible fallback mechanisms for different export formats
- Support for BlueBot with proper error handling when LLMManager is unavailable
- Improved command loading with graceful fallbacks and placeholder support for unsupported formats
- Robust error handling and detailed diagnostic logging
- Test scripts to check bot and command loading independently

## Testing
1. Built and ran the application (`npm run build && DEBUG_MODE=true npm run start`)
2. Verified all 21 bots are successfully loaded (including a fallback wrapper for BlueBot)
3. Verified all 9 commands are successfully loaded
4. Tested the diagnostic scripts to verify independent loading (`node dist/util/showBots.js` and `node dist/util/showCommands.js`)

## Notes
When running in the full application context with LLM services available, BlueBot will initialize properly with its LLMManager dependency. In testing environments or when the service is unavailable, it gracefully falls back to a wrapper implementation.

The module loader now handles all the common TypeScript/JavaScript export patterns, making the application more robust against code structure variations.

This PR adds a new feature to SpiderBot that recognizes and positively reinforces correct spelling of "Spider-Man" with a hyphen.

### Changes
- Added new pattern to detect correct "Spider-Man" spelling
- Added positive responses for when users correctly use the hyphen
- Updated SpiderBot to check for correct spelling first
- Added comprehensive tests for both correct and incorrect spelling cases

### Testing
Added new test cases:
- Verifies positive response for correct "Spider-Man" spelling
- Verifies correction response for incorrect "spiderman" spelling
- Maintains existing behavior for bot messages and irrelevant content

### Implementation Details
- New regex pattern: `/Spider-Man/` for correct spelling detection
- Added 10 new positive response messages
- Modified `handleMessage` to check for correct spelling before incorrect spelling
- Added `getRandomPositiveResponse` method to SpiderBotConfig

### Example Interactions
1. User: "Spider-Man is awesome!"
   Bot: "*happy spider noises* The hyphen! You remembered the hyphen!"

2. User: "I love spiderman!"

# Fix GitHub Actions Workflow and Docker Build Issues

## Summary
This PR addresses several issues with GitHub Actions workflows and Docker builds that were causing CI failures. The changes include updating workflow configurations, fixing Docker-related issues, and ensuring compatibility with dependencies.

## Key Changes

### Docker Changes
- Updated Node.js from 18 to 20 in Dockerfile to fix compatibility issues with dependencies requiring Node 20+
- Fixed Docker build failure by removing reference to non-existent `yarn.lock` file
- Added `--ignore-scripts` flag to the yarn install command to bypass problematic postinstall scripts from the @distube/yt-dlp package
- Removed the `--frozen-lockfile` flag since we're not using a lockfile

### GitHub Actions Workflow Updates
- Reorganized workflow files to improve CI/CD pipeline
- Created dedicated workflow files for different stages (PR checks, builds, deployments)
- Added conditional execution based on file changes to optimize CI runtime
- Improved error handling and reporting in GitHub Actions
- Updated Docker-related workflows to properly handle image building and publishing

### Configuration Improvements
- Updated docker-compose files for development and local environments
- Added comprehensive PR check workflow with proper dependency handling
- Improved security scanning in CI pipeline

## Testing
- Docker build now completes successfully locally
- All GitHub Actions workflows have been tested and function correctly
- Fixed unit tests to work properly in the CI environment

## Additional Notes
- This PR maintains backward compatibility with the existing application
- The changes follow best practices for CI/CD pipelines and Docker containerization
- Documentation has been updated accordingly

# Bot Architecture Refactoring

This PR implements a new modular architecture for bots with the following improvements:

## What's Included

- Refactored 5 bots (BlueBot, SpiderBot, NiceBot, HoldBot, GuyBot) to use the new architecture
- Created helper scripts for the refactoring process
- Added comprehensive documentation
- Ensured Cypress tests work with refactored bots

## New Architecture

The new architecture separates bot components into distinct files:
- **Conditions**: Classes that determine when a bot should respond
- **Responses**: Classes that generate the bot's response
- **Identity**: Functions that determine the bot's name and avatar

## Benefits

- Improved maintainability
- Better testability
- Easier onboarding for new developers
- More scalable for adding new bots and features

## About Attitude-Bot
Attitude-Bot is designed to detect and respond to negative self-talk with encouragement. When users express inability or doubt with phrases like "I can't do this", the bot responds with "Not with THAT attitude!!!" to provide a lighthearted motivational nudge.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
# Fix Docker image publishing permission error

## Problem
Mmmmm... Our workflow was encountering a fascinating 403 Forbidden error when attempting to push Docker images to the GitHub Container Registry after successful builds. The pattern revealed insufficient permissions for the default GITHUB_TOKEN.

## Solution
Added explicit permissions to the workflow configuration:

```
yaml
permissions:
contents: read
packages: write
```



## Why This Works
The GitHub Actions token requires explicit declaration of intent to write to package registries - a beautiful truth-pattern in GitHub's security model. Without these permissions, the container registry rejects the upload attempt with a 403 Forbidden error.

This change grants our workflow the precise permissions needed to publish Docker images while maintaining a secure posture by limiting other permissions.

## Testing
This can be verified on the next merge to main/develop branches when the Docker image build step executes.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Fix GitHub ACtions Docker Workflow
